questions.q: Kanshi Ram is best known as the founder of which political party?
questions.explanation: Kanshi Ram founded the Bahujan Samaj Party in 1984.

questions.q: Kanshi Ram's political work focused on mobilising which sections of society?
questions.explanation: He worked to mobilise marginalised and historically disadvantaged communities.

questions.q: Kanshi Ram is primarily associated with the cause of:
questions.explanation: He is recognised for his focus on social justice and the political empowerment of marginalised groups.

questions.q: In which Indian state was Kanshi Ram born?
questions.explanation: Kanshi Ram was born in Punjab.

questions.q: The party Kanshi Ram founded is commonly abbreviated as:
questions.explanation: The Bahujan Samaj Party is commonly abbreviated as BSP.

questions.q: Kanshi Ram is best described as a:
questions.explanation: He was a political organiser who built movements and a party for marginalised communities.

questions.q: Before entering full-time politics, Kanshi Ram worked as an employee in which sector?
questions.explanation: He worked at a government defence research and scientific establishment before turning to political activism.

questions.q: Kanshi Ram's politics drew inspiration from the ideas of which constitutional figure?
questions.explanation: His movement drew heavily on the ideas and legacy of Dr. B.R. Ambedkar.

questions.q: Kanshi Ram's organising work was strongest in which large northern Indian state?
questions.explanation: His party became a major political force in Uttar Pradesh.

questions.q: Kanshi Ram is associated with the political idea of empowering the 'Bahujan,' a term referring to:
options: The majority made up of marginalised communities
questions.explanation: 'Bahujan' refers to the large body of marginalised and majority communities his movement sought to unite.

questions.q: In which year did Kanshi Ram found the Bahujan Samaj Party?
questions.explanation: He founded the Bahujan Samaj Party in 1984.

questions.q: In which year was Kanshi Ram born?
questions.explanation: Kanshi Ram was born on 15 March 1934.

questions.q: Before founding the BSP, Kanshi Ram established an employees' organisation known by which name?
questions.explanation: He founded BAMCEF, an organisation of educated employees from marginalised communities, in the 1970s.

questions.q: The acronym BAMCEF, the organisation Kanshi Ram founded, stands for an association of which group?
questions.explanation: BAMCEF stood for the All India Backward and Minority Communities Employees' Federation.

questions.q: Kanshi Ram founded a political action organisation in 1981, prior to the BSP, often referred to by which initials?
questions.explanation: In 1981 he launched DS-4 (Dalit Shoshit Samaj Sangharsh Samiti) as a precursor to the BSP.

questions.q: In which year did Kanshi Ram pass away?
questions.explanation: Kanshi Ram died on 9 October 2006.

questions.q: Kanshi Ram was first elected to the Lok Sabha from a constituency in which state?
questions.explanation: He won a Lok Sabha seat from a constituency in Uttar Pradesh.

questions.q: Kanshi Ram's successor as leader of the Bahujan Samaj Party was:
questions.explanation: Mayawati succeeded Kanshi Ram as the leader of the Bahujan Samaj Party.

questions.q: Kanshi Ram authored a notable booklet critiquing existing social structures, titled:
questions.explanation: He authored the booklet 'The Chamcha Age' setting out his political analysis.

questions.q: Kanshi Ram's home government job before activism was located in which city?
questions.explanation: He worked at a government scientific establishment in Pune before leaving to organise politically.

questions.q: The Bahujan Samaj Party founded by Kanshi Ram contests elections using which kind of symbol allotted by the Election Commission?
questions.explanation: The BSP's election symbol is the elephant.

questions.q: Kanshi Ram's political movement is generally associated with which broad goal in the electoral arena?
options: Increasing political representation of marginalised communities
questions.explanation: His movement sought greater electoral and political representation for marginalised communities.

questions.q: Kanshi Ram founded BAMCEF in which year?
questions.explanation: He formally founded BAMCEF in 1978 after years of preparatory organising.

questions.q: The full name of the DS-4 organisation Kanshi Ram launched in 1981 translates to a committee for the struggle of which society?
questions.explanation: DS-4 stood for Dalit Shoshit Samaj Sangharsh Samiti, a committee for the struggle of the oppressed and exploited society.

questions.q: Kanshi Ram first won election to the Lok Sabha in which year?
questions.explanation: He was first elected to the Lok Sabha in 1991.

questions.q: Kanshi Ram's political strategy emphasised converting social strength into which measurable resource?
questions.explanation: He famously stressed translating numerical social strength into votes and political power.

questions.q: Kanshi Ram belonged to which religious community by birth?
questions.explanation: He was born into a Sikh family in Punjab.

questions.q: The Bahujan Samaj Party under Kanshi Ram first came to share power in the government of which state in the 1990s?
questions.explanation: The BSP first entered government through coalition arrangements in Uttar Pradesh in the 1990s.

questions.q: Kanshi Ram is often credited with popularising a slogan emphasising the numerical strength of marginalised communities expressed as a percentage. That figure was commonly cited as:
questions.explanation: He popularised the idea that the bahujan made up roughly eighty-five percent of the population.

questions.q: Kanshi Ram's organising career began in earnest in which decade, after he left his government job?
questions.explanation: He launched his full-time organising work in the 1970s after resigning from government service.
